flashcards.definition: A graph plotting a process statistic over time with upper and lower control limits (UCL/LCL) to detect out-of-control processes.
Inspecting a random sample from a lot and deciding to accept or reject the entire lot based on the results.
A quality methodology targeting ≤ 3.4 defects per million opportunities; the process mean is 6 standard deviations from specifications.
Upper and Lower Control Limits, typically set at μ ± 3σ on a control chart.
A measure comparing the allowable spread (specification range) to the actual process spread (6σ).
